name: CRDT
def: Conflict-free Replicated Data Type: a structure replicas can update independently and merge without conflict.
problem: Allow concurrent, offline-tolerant edits across replicas that converge automatically — no central coordination.
mechanism: Operations/states are designed to be commutative, associative and idempotent so merges are deterministic; state-based CRDTs merge via a join (least upper bound), op-based via commutative ops.
limits: Garbage collection of history is hard
failures: Semantics that "converge" but not to user intent
whenNot: Strong consistency / invariants across keys required
myths: "CRDTs remove all conflicts" — they remove merge conflicts, not semantic surprises

complexity.Note: Merge O(state); metadata overhead varies by type
